_patch_meta.description: Dimension review patch — C16: 135 wa_dimension_index updates (dimension + dominant_subject + dimension_confidence); 5 wa_session_b_findings inserts; 2 wa_session_research_flags inserts

set.notes: Positively valenced felt inner state: fullness, satisfaction, delight in the satisfied soul
God's favour and blessing directed toward person — inner standing grounded in divine relational act
Opening of inner perception = inner change in what can be known; anointing as transformative gift
Pride corrupting originally exalted moral standing — stable inner quality; UNSEEN (cherubic/angelic figure)
Spirit-reception producing inner change — paradigmatic transformation: Spirit rushing upon and transforming inner life
Inner moral orientation of reverence for divine appointment — stable prohibition reflecting moral character
God exercising capacity to appoint and commission — the act that designates and marks divine favour
Oil as sacred medium; inner-being content is the consecrated state enacted. No dominant human subject — NONE.
H4888B variant — same content and dimension as 169-001 (H4888A). Oil as sacred medium; NONE dominant.
Priest's moral condition (sin/purity) as inner-being substance carrying covenantal weight
Set-apart identity and moral standing before God — stable quality of the anointed king
Messiah as exercise of divine authority and fulfilment of God's sovereign purpose. Automated Volitional/Will incorrect — reassigned.
Grief as negatively valenced felt inner state expressed outwardly through abstention from anointing
God as agent of inner restoration — claiming and beautifying what was degraded
Perceptual restoration = inner change. Automated Volitional/Will incorrect — this is Transformation.
QA-VAGUE: description names inner quality and character broadly — Moral Character is broadest defensible assignment
Joy as positively valenced inner state — oil of gladness
Inner consecration as stable set-apart quality — oil as outward sign of inner state
Disordered stable inner quality — moral character in negative register: self-indulgence and spiritual unfaithfulness
Oil penetrating to deepest interior — metaphor for inner-being reaching and transforming
Inner orientation of love and devotion toward Christ — anticipatory act of anointing in preparation for burial
Spirit of denial as stable negative moral character — UNSEEN (spirit of antichrist). Automated Cognitive/Mind reassigned.
God as agent of negative inner transformation — causing the heart to become dull and unresponsive (judicial hardening)
Inner movement from dependence to self-sufficiency — abundance producing inner dulling and turning from God
God as agent of erasure/forgiveness — inner change in standing before God through blotting out of sin
God as eschatological agent of inner reversal — final wiping away of all inner suffering in renewed creation
Inner orientation of love and honour toward Christ expressed through costly devotion
Satisfaction as positively valenced felt inner state — inner fullness of delight when nourished by God's provision
Flourishing and vitality as positively valenced inner state — sustained through trust in God. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Spirit's anointing as inner knowing/teaching — remains and teaches from within, guarding against deception. Automated Cognitive/Mind maps to Cognition.
G5545B variant — same content as 65-001. Spirit's inner teaching and knowing function.
Faith as inner relational orientation toward Christ as ground of life. Automated Identity/Selfhood reassigned — the inner-being content is the person's faith-orientation, not identity per se.
Union with Christ = inner transformation; being in Christ constitutes a new inner state
Inner life resting on received truth of Christ's death and resurrection — posture of trust and dependence on what is outside the self
Jesus anointed with Spirit for ministry — divine capacity given for exercise. Automated Volitional/Capacity reassigned to Agency/Power.
Inner certainty/firmness grounded in God's anointing act — security from outside the self
Inner regard, love, and honour directed toward another — expressed through anointing
Faith and intercession as inner posture of reliance — prayer as dependence on God for healing. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Holy standing and inner dedication as stable inner quality — crown as outward sign. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Rupture of consecration = inner change of standing — movement from dedicated to rejected state
Directed inner act of willing consecration or separation — core volitional act of self-dedication or rejection
Voluntary inner commitment of total dedication — Nazirite vow as outward form of inner volitional act
Covenant loyalty as stable inner orientation — dedication as expression of what the person is before God
God's holiness as stable divine character quality — manifested before nations. Automated Theological/Divine-Human maps to Moral Character. GOD dominant.
Reverence and awe as inner orientation called forth by the sanctuary — how the person faces God in the sacred space. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Inner readiness, solemnity, and responsibility as moral qualities required for approach to the Holy Place. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Inner wisdom and discernment as primary content — Spirit is agent; human person is subject of the resulting knowing function. Automated Cognitive/Mind maps to Cognition.
Inner identity constituted by holy belonging — stable moral quality of the saints of the Most High. Automated Identity/Selfhood maps to Moral Character.
Holy character, orientation, and standing before God as defining quality — Moral Character in its fullest form. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Moral hypocrisy and covenantal violation — stable inner quality in negative register. Automated Moral/Conscience maps to Moral Character.
Purified inner condition as criterion for participation — holiness as inner quality defining access
The consecrated state itself — stable inner quality of dedication; whole person oriented toward God
God exercising capacity to set apart — the constituting act that makes persons holy. Automated Identity/Selfhood reassigned to Agency/Power (names what God does, not the outcome).
Voluntary self-dedication — the inner act of orienting the whole person toward God. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Inner orientation toward God in time — facing God through the Sabbath rhythm of sacred rest and encounter
Holiness as inner moral quality — set apart inwardly from defilement. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Core Moral Character group — holiness as inner moral character and set-apart orientation by which person is consecrated to God. Automated Moral/Conscience maps to Moral Character.
Reverence, worship, and desire evoked by encountering God's holiness — inner orientation toward God. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Spirit's grief and placement within people — relational disposition of God toward his people. GOD dominant. Automated Relational/Social maps to Relational Disposition.
Sanctification as transformative process — holiness as goal of Christian life and fruit of Spirit's work. Automated Moral/Conscience reassigned; the process of arriving is Transformation.
Holiness as inner moral quality actively pursued and established in the heart — the quality itself. Automated Moral/Conscience maps to Moral Character.
Constitutive inner character quality — holiness paired with righteousness as what the new person is. Automated Moral/Conscience maps to Moral Character.
Moral character as imperative — be holy as God is holy; inner life grounded in God's character. Automated Moral/Conscience maps to Moral Character.
Diverse inner orientations toward the Holy One — trust, awe, joy, rejection, unwillingness. Relational Disposition captures the directedness. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Set-apart standing as stable inner identity quality — shapes who the person is before God. Automated Identity/Selfhood maps to Moral Character.
Inner transformation toward sharing divine character — person disciplined to become partaker of God's holiness
Misplaced inner relational stance — allegiance, rejoicing, and worship wrongly directed toward idol. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Conscience and moral formation as inner-being content — incompatibility of idol and God within the person. Automated Moral/Conscience maps to Moral Character.
Conscience as inner moral faculty governing behaviour — food sacrificed to idols as conscience crisis. Automated Moral/Conscience maps to Moral Character.
Defining inner orientation toward created things rather than God — misdirected allegiance as relational stance
Conscience vulnerability — weak conscience damaged by exposure to idol's temple. Automated Moral/Conscience maps to Moral Character.
Inner response disclosing orientation toward God's honour — provocation reveals relational stance
Ultimate inner allegiance misdirected — heart's displacement of God by created things. Core Relational Disposition group for idolatry. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Spirit-given capacity to receive and speak divine disclosure — the gift exercised. Automated Volitional/Capacity maps to Agency/Power.
Faithful inner response required toward divinely authorised word — inner orientation toward Scripture/prophecy
Inner disposition moved toward courage/action or manipulated — volitional direction as target of the prophetic word. Automated Character/Disposition reassigned to Volition.
Inner orientation toward God as defining quality of the prophetic person — shapes their speech. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Endurance under persecution as stable inner quality — what the authentic prophet is, not a momentary feeling
Scripture orienting the inner person toward God's purposes — inner relational direction via the prophetic canon. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Human person exercising Spirit-given capacity in the act of prophesying
Communal exercise of Spirit-gift for building up the inner life of congregation. Automated Volitional/Will maps to Agency/Power.
QA-THIN: testing authenticity of prophetic speech — moral quality of integrity/authenticity at stake
Predatory and deceptive inner character masked outwardly — stable negative moral quality of the false prophet
Eschatological False Prophet misorienting others' inner allegiance toward the beast. UNSEEN dominant. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Prophetic word as inner-being lamp — illuminates inner life until Christ dawns within; inner knowing function
Inner calling vs false claim to prophetic authority — moral character of authenticity/integrity
Spirit activating inward responsive capacity — inner capacity exercised in prophesying. Automated Volitional/Capacity maps to Agency/Power.
True inner commission and orientation toward God expressed in prophetic speech. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Inner disposition of falsehood — false prophets speaking from their own hearts. Stable negative moral character.
Obedient inner responsiveness to God's word — relational orientation toward God's authority expressed in directed speech. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Exercise of prophetic/mediating capacity — prophetic guidance, worship leadership. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Sustaining inner disposition of hearers toward covenantal action — volitional direction as target. Automated Character/Disposition maps to Volition.
Stable inner orientation of rebellion — seeking supernatural power outside God; work of flesh / spiritual apostasy
Inner orientation of rejection toward God — engaging occult power as the directional act of turning from God's sole authority
Sorcery as relational substitute — occult means substituted for God = misdirected relational stance. Automated Moral/Conscience reassigned.
Sorcerer as character type — inner-spiritual orientation defined by occult practice; who the person is
Inner heart-orientation toward divine testimony — how person faces/relates to God's word: inclined, kept, ground of renewal
Spiritual condition (having or lacking dawn) as criterion for reception — inner quality determining receptivity
Belief/unbelief as inner volitional response to signs — the act of choosing to believe or refuse when confronted with miraculous evidence
Conscience as inner moral faculty addressed by word dwelling within. Automated Moral/Conscience maps to Moral Character.
Inner integrity in the act of witnessing — moral quality at stake in true vs false witness. Automated Moral/Conscience maps to Moral Character.
Inner orientation called toward repentance and receptivity — divine warning directing the inner person toward God
Joy, delight, and steadfast love as positively valenced inner states evoked by the testimonies — primary inner-being content is affective
Inner faithfulness and steadfastness as stable qualities — not forgetting the law despite external constraint
QA-VAGUE: unbelief as inner volitional state — refusal or failure to believe; the heart described, rebuked, or wrestled with
QA-VAGUE: unbelief as persistent inner volitional state with decisive consequences at communal scale
Wilful inner refusal — paradigmatic volitional act of disobedience toward God, his Son, or his word. Automated Spiritual/God-ward reassigned.
Hardness of mind as stable negative inner quality — persistent state producing communal consequences. Automated Relational/Social reassigned.
QA-THIN: dē intensifying divine command — Agency/Power of God exercising authority. Automated Volitional/Will reassigned; command originates in GOD.
Inner act of judging and distinguishing — moral discernment as knowing/perceiving function. Automated Moral/Conscience reassigned; the act of distinguishing is Cognition.
Inner volitional division — heart split between faith and hesitation, driven and tossed; core doubt group

Violent inner states (wrath, predatory character, destructive anger) as negatively valenced. HUMAN is dominant experiential subject. GOD-dimension (divine wrath/resolve) present — see Session B pointer DIM-191-001. Automated Theological/Divine-Human reassigned.
Inner conviction and truthfulness — the 'truly' anchoring moral assertion and confession about God's character. Automated Moral/Conscience maps to Moral Character.
Wavering as volitional instability — faith faltering between trust and hesitation, addressed directly by Jesus
Certainty, conviction, and resolve as stable inner qualities — character of apostolic conviction and wholehearted commitment
Inner spiritual capacity to distinguish good from evil and spirits — knowing/discerning function. Automated Cognitive/Mind maps to Cognition.
Impartiality as stable inner character quality of wisdom from above — absence of wavering or prejudice. Automated Cognitive/Mind reassigned; this is a character quality, not an act of knowing.
Predatory inner character of unjust leaders as primary content — moral character in negative register; God's resolve also present but human leaders' character is dominant
Inner integrity under test — person refusing defilement, bearing losses rather than shifting blame
Inner recognition of grace and new beginning — positively valenced inner state of hope renewed by the olive leaf sign
Constant inner orientation toward God — always praying, giving thanks, rejoicing, abounding; mark of life wholly directed toward God
God as Almighty — absolute power as object of worship and ground of covenant; divine sovereignty over history. GOD dominant.
Universal divine mandate — gospel preached everywhere; inner-being mandate with no geographic boundary. GOD dominant as source of the commission.
Complete sufficiency of Christ's saving power as Agency/Power — the human's complete inability is the contrast. GOD (Christ) dominant.
Inner experience of complete encirclement and inescapable judgment — negatively valenced felt inner state
Inner orientation of seeking directed toward Jesus from every quarter — relational orientation of universal drawing
Inner act of consecration and thankfulness directed toward God — how person faces God in pronouncing blessing
Exercise of prophetic/covenantal capacity in pronouncing blessing over persons and communities
Counterintuitive orientation of blessing persecutors — stable inner quality of grace and non-retaliation in the face of curse
God's inner disposition of love as source of sovereign covenantal blessing — Relational Disposition with GOD dominant
Human inner-being orientation of worship, doxology, and praise toward God — how person faces God in blessing
Exercise of covenantal/prophetic capacity — declaring favour, invoking God's goodness over persons and communities
Inverted inner orientation — moral hypocrisy of blessing used against its proper object; self-blessing of the sinful heart
Blessedness as positively valenced felt inner state — receiving God's grace. Automated Affective/Emotional maps to Emotion — Positive.
God-given capacity exercised for building up the community — charisma as Spirit-gift exercised. Automated Volitional/Capacity maps to Agency/Power.

record.finding: Registry 6 (anointing) spans 7 dimensions across 38 groups — the widest dimensional range in C16. Session B should examine whether this breadth coheres around a unifying theme (Spirit-mediated transformation and divine endowment) or represents genuinely distinct inner-being phenomena requiring separate analytical threads.

operations.description: Session B pointer — anointing: dimensional diversity warrants unifying theme examination

record.finding: Multiple Transformation groups in Reg 6 have GOD as dominant subject (167-001, 179-002, 4697-001, 4701-001, 4701-002). Session B should explore the full theology of God-initiated inner transformation in the anointing vocabulary — spanning Spirit-impartation, forgiveness, restoration, eschatological renewal, and judicial dulling/hardening as distinct expressions of the same divine transforming agency.

operations.description: Session B pointer — anointing: GOD-initiated transformation distribution across group types

record.finding: Group 1286-001 (H2963 ta.raph, tearing) was assigned Emotion — Negative / HUMAN to capture the human experience of violent inner states: wrath, predatory character, destructive anger, and the suffering of being torn. The same group contains significant GOD-dimension content — divine wrath and fierce resolve to fight for his people. Session B should study both dimensions: (1) the human inner-being experience of violent states and of being torn under judgment or hostile power; and (2) the divine inner-being reality of wrath and fierce protective resolve, exploring whether these are the same phenomenon viewed from different subjects or whether they name distinct inner-being realities.

operations.description: Session B pointer — doubt/taraph: dual-subject violent inner states — human experience and divine wrath both require Session B study

record.finding: Registry 124 (prophecy) shows a consistent pattern of Agency/Power (Spirit-enabled capacity exercised) alongside Relational Disposition (inner orientation toward God or his word). Session B should examine the relationship between inner capacity and inner orientation in the prophetic person: is prophetic capacity a dimension in its own right, or is it always constituted by and in service of a prior relational orientation toward God?

operations.description: Session B pointer — prophecy: relationship between Agency/Power and Relational Disposition in prophetic identity

record.finding: The blessing vocabulary reveals a mirror structure in Relational Disposition: group 1299-001 names God's inner disposition of love as the source of his blessing toward persons (GOD dominant); group 1299-002 names the human inner orientation of worship and praise as the act of blessing God (HUMAN dominant). Session B should examine this mutual relational structure — whether the blessing vocabulary discloses a covenantal reciprocity in inner-being orientation between God and persons, and what this reveals about the inner being as the site of covenant relationship.

operations.description: Session B pointer — blessing: mirror Relational Disposition structure — God toward persons, persons toward God

record.description: C16 contains a substantial concentration of GOD-dominant Agency/Power and Transformation groups across anointing, consecration, and blessing — God appointing, transforming, restoring, forgiving, and blessing as inner-being acts directed toward the human person. Session D should synthesise how God's inner-being activity toward persons is distributed across the full programme and whether C16 represents a distinct domain of divine inner-being engagement (endowment/consecration) distinguishable from other clusters.

operations.description: Session D pointer — GOD-dominant inner-being activity in C16 across anointing, consecration, blessing

record.description: Group 910-001 (hagiotēs, holiness) names holiness as a divine character quality that the human person is disciplined to share — inner transformation making the person a partaker of God's own holiness. This group is a potential locus for Session D exploration of the imago dei as inner-being reality: the human inner being shaped toward the character of God. Session D should trace connections to clusters carrying righteousness, faithfulness, and love to assess whether a coherent imago dei dimension-pattern emerges across the programme.

operations.description: Session D pointer — holiness/hagiotēs: imago dei as inner-being reality — partaking of God's character across clusters
